subject: DevOps
title: GitOps for Infrastructure
description: A micro-lesson that makes GitOps for Infrastructure usable.

hook.text: A developer runs terraform apply from their laptop. It works. But nobody else saw the change or approved it.

buildup.text: GitOps means all changes go through Git. No local applies. Pull request, review, approve, merge, auto-apply.

discovery.text: CI/CD runs terraform plan on pull requests. The plan output appears as a comment. Reviewers see exactly what changes.

twist.text: On merge, CI/CD runs terraform apply automatically. The merge commit is the audit trail. No manual intervention.

climax.text: Atlantis and Spacelift are tools built specifically for GitOps-driven Terraform. They enforce the workflow.

punchline.text: GitOps for IaC: review, approve, merge, auto-apply. No manual applies.

quiz.question: What does a GitOps workflow for IaC enforce?
options: All changes go through pull requests with review before auto-apply
All infrastructure is managed from the command line only
Changes are applied immediately without review
